reasoning: The iPhone 18 is expected to launch in September 2026. As of April 2026, rumors from the most credible supply chain analysts and insiders (such as Ming-Chi Kuo and Mark Gurman) detail the upcoming lineage (including variable aperture, 2nm chips, and even foldables), but there are no credible reports of a portless model. In fact, Mark Gurman reported that Apple previously considered a portless design for the iPhone 17 Air but abandoned it. Furthermore, credible tech news outlets (Macworld, AppleInsider, PCMag) have explicitly debunked recent rumors of a portless iPhone 18, noting that they stem entirely from a viral AI-generated hoax on TikTok. The presence of EU regulations mandating USB-C and the practical limitations of current wireless charging technology provide strong fundamental reasons against a portless transition this year. Given the high reliability of hardware leaks 5 months prior to an iPhone launch, the absence of real rumors implies an overwhelmingly low probability. The Manifold market was at 7% earlier in April, but based on the comprehensive debunking of the TikTok hoax and the solidifying consensus around the iPhone 18 features, the true probability is even lower, around 3%.
